title: Smoky French Fish Stew
description: A comforting, umami-packed fish stew with a gentle smoky kick, ideal for a hangover cure and pescatarian gatherings.

ingredients.name: fish stock
ingredients.notes: preferably homemade with smoked fish bones

ingredients.name: firm white fish fillets (cod or haddock), cubed

instructions: Heat 1 tbsp of olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add diced onion, minced garlic, carrot, and celery; sauté 5 minutes until softened.
Stir in smoked paprika, salt, pepper, and dried thyme; cook 1 minute for aroma release.
Pour in fish stock, apple cider vinegar, and crushed tomatoes; bring to a gentle simmer.
Add the cubed fish to the pot, reduce heat to low, and cover; simmer 12-15 minutes, or until fish flakes easily.
Whisk in remaining 1 tbsp olive oil and smoked sea salt; adjust seasoning if needed.

Remove from heat and stir in chopped parsley.
Serve hot with lemon wedges on the side for squeezing over the stew.
Enjoy the warm, smoky flavors that soothe both palate and hangover.
